name: Pueblo Barbecued Pork Roast
recipeIngredient: 4 large Ripe tomatoes, quartered and seeded
1 tbsp Ground New Mexican red chile
1 Dried medium-hot New Mexican red chile, crushed
4 lb To 5 lb Pork rib roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a large heavy saucepan and sauté onions over medium heat until soft.
2. Add garlic, juniper berries, coriander seed, and bay leaf; sauté for 2 to 3 minutes longer.
3. Add tomatoes, water, cider vinegar, honey, ground New Mexican red chile, crushed red chile, and salt.
4. Simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
5. Stir in grated chocolate and simmer, uncovered, for an additional 20 to 30 minutes until fairly thick.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. Place the pork roast fat side up in a roasting pan and generously baste with the sauce.
8. Roast for about 3 hours, basting occasionally with the sauce and pan drippings.
9. Let the roast rest for 10 minutes before carving.
10. Slice the roast and spoon additional sauce over each portion.
